name: SQLGlot
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); no hosted service

auth.notes: Pure Python library; no auth required

performance.notes: Pure Python parsing; complex queries 10-50ms; transpilation adds minimal overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Python SQL parser, transpiler, and optimizer. Supports 20+ SQL dialects (BigQuery, Snowflake, Spark, Postgres, etc.). Parses SQL to AST, transforms, and generates dialect-specific SQL. Used in production by Airbnb, Netflix, Robinhood. Excellent for agents that need to understand, transform, or validate SQL queries. Also includes SQL executor for testing.

gotchas: Dialect coverage varies — some edge cases in less common SQL dialects
Not a database driver — only for SQL parsing/transformation, not execution
Large SQL files can be slow to parse in pure Python
best_when: Agents that generate, validate, or transform SQL queries across dialects
Data pipeline tools needing dialect-agnostic SQL representation
SQL migration tools moving between databases (Postgres → BigQuery, etc.)
Code analysis tools detecting SQL injection or query quality issues
avoid_when: Non-Python environments needing native performance (use ANTLR or native parsers)
Simple SQL formatting only (use sqlfluff or pg_format)
summary: SQLGlot is a Python SQL parser, transpiler, and optimizer supporting 20+ SQL dialects. Parse SQL to AST, transform it, and generate dialect-specific SQL (e.g., Postgres → BigQuery). Production-proven at Airbnb, Netflix, Robinhood. Excellent for agents that need to understand or transform SQL. Type-annotated, well-documented, highly reliable. Best Python-native SQL manipulation library available.
